name: TAO Delayed Retest Continuation v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger. The edge should come from participating only after reclaimed structure proves itself, not from buying the initial spike.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest continuations still zero-trade, if retests fail to hold often enough, or if delayed entry arrives after the move is already spent.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks local structure, retests the breakout zone, and continues only if the retest holds rather than fully reverting into the old range.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retest logic should avoid first-bar fakeouts while remaining denser than elaborate multi-stage confirmation chains.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful because breakout and retest structure is expressive on a volatile asset, giving the delayed-entry logic room to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO remains fully range-bound, delayed retests may simply mean failed breakouts with no continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but gives back too much after first target, simplify the back half into faster scale-outs before altering entry structure.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest, hold, and participation filter are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept manageable by broad retest-zone logic instead of exact candle choreography

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
